# Device Alarm Notifications

> Process hardware alarm codes embedded in device position transmissions, generate individual alert events per alarm type (SOS, tamper, vibration, accident, jamming, etc.), and route notifications to subscribed users.

**Category:** Notification · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · alarm · sos · panic · tamper · safety · fleet · alert

## What this does

Process hardware alarm codes embedded in device position transmissions, generate individual alert events per alarm type (SOS, tamper, vibration, accident, jamming, etc.), and route notifications to subscribed users.

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **alarm** *(text, optional)*
- **alarm_type** *(select, optional)*
- **suppress_duplicates** *(boolean, optional)*

## What must be true

- **0:** A position may carry multiple simultaneous alarm codes; each alarm code generates a separate alert event
- **1:** If suppress_duplicates is enabled, an alarm code that was already present in the previous position does not generate a new event; only newly appearing codes trigger alerts
- **2:** Each alarm event is linked to the position where it was detected, preserving exact coordinates and time
- **3:** Alarm events can be routed to users via any configured notification channel (push, email, SMS, webhook)
- **4:** Alarm codes are device and protocol specific; the platform normalises known codes to standard identifiers

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **No Alarm Code** — when position does not carry any alarm attribute, then No alarm event generated for this position.
- **Duplicate Alarm Suppressed** — when alarm code is present in both the current and previous position; suppress_duplicates eq true, then Duplicate alarm suppressed; no redundant alert generated.
- **Alarm Event Generated** — when position carries one or more alarm codes in the alarm attribute; suppress_duplicates is false OR the alarm code was not present in the previous position, then Alarm event stored; notification handlers dispatch alerts to subscribed users.

## Errors it can return

- `ALARM_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced in the position record does not exist

## Connects to

- **gps-position-ingestion**
- **device-status-tracking**
- **fleet-device-sharing**
